children.text: The State shall adopt and carry out a program for testing and ensuring the fitness of individuals to operate commercial motor vehicles consistent with the minimum standards prescribed by the Secretary of Transportation under .section 31305(a) of this title

The State may issue a commercial driver’s license to an individual only if the individual passes written and driving tests for the operation of a commercial motor vehicle that comply with the minimum standards.

The State shall have in effect and enforce a law providing that an individual with a blood alcohol concentration level at or above the level established by when operating a commercial motor vehicle is deemed to be driving under the influence of alcohol.section 31310(a) of this title

The State shall authorize an individual to operate a commercial motor vehicle only by issuing a commercial driver’s license containing the information described in .section 31308(3) of this title1See References in Text note below.1

Not later than the time period prescribed by the Secretary by regulation, the State shall notify the Secretary or the operator of the information system under , as the case may be, of the proposed issuance of the license and other information the Secretary may require to ensure identification of the individual applying for the license.section 31309 of this title

Before issuing a commercial driver’s license to an individual or renewing such a license, the State shall request from any other State that has issued a driver’s license to the individual all information about the driving record of the individual.

Not later than 30 days after issuing a commercial driver’s license, the State shall notify the Secretary or the operator of the information system under , as the case may be, of the issuance.section 31309 of this title

Not later than 10 days after disqualifying the holder of a commercial driver’s license from operating a commercial motor vehicle (or after revoking, suspending, or canceling the license) for at least 60 days, the State shall notify the Secretary or the operator of the information system under , as the case may be, and the State that issued the license, of the disqualification, revocation, suspension, or cancellation, and the violation that resulted in the disqualification, revocation, suspension, or cancellation shall be recorded.section 31309 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: has a commercial driver’s license issued by another State; or

children.label: B
children.text: is operating a commercial vehicle without a commercial driver’s license and has a driver’s license issued by another State,

children.label: A
children.text: The State may not issue a commercial driver’s license to an individual during a period in which the individual is disqualified from operating a commercial motor vehicle or the individual’s driver’s license is revoked, suspended, or canceled.

children.label: i
children.text: the individual is disqualified from operating a commercial motor vehicle; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the individual’s driver’s license is revoked, suspended, or canceled.

The State may issue a commercial driver’s license to an individual who has a commercial driver’s license issued by another State only if the individual first returns the driver’s license issued by the other State.

children.label: A
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C), the State may issue a commercial driver’s license only to an individual who operates or will operate a commercial motor vehicle and is domiciled in the State.

children.label: i
children.text: operates or will operate a commercial motor vehicle; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is not domiciled in a State that issues commercial driver’s licenses.

children.label: i
children.text: operates or will operate a commercial motor vehicle;

children.label: I
children.text: the armed forces (as that term is defined in ); orsection 101(a) of title 10

children.label: II
children.text: the reserve components (as that term is defined in ); andsection 31305(d)(2) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: is not domiciled in the State, but whose temporary or permanent duty station is located in the State.
The State shall impose penalties consistent with this chapter that the State considers appropriate and the Secretary approves for an individual operating a commercial motor vehicle.

children.label: A
children.text: the individual has a commercial driver’s license issued by another State under the minimum standards prescribed by the Secretary under ;section 31305(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the license is not revoked, suspended, or canceled; and

children.label: C
children.text: the individual is not disqualified from operating a commercial motor vehicle.

The State shall disqualify an individual from operating a commercial motor vehicle for the same reasons and time periods for which the Secretary shall disqualify the individual under subsections (b)–(e), (i)(1)(A) and (i)(2) of section 31310.

children.label: i
children.text: has been disqualified from operating a motor vehicle (except a commercial motor vehicle);

children.label: ii
children.text: has had a license (except a license authorizing the individual to operate a commercial motor vehicle) revoked, suspended, or canceled for cause in the 3-year period ending on the date of application for the commercial driver’s license; or

children.label: iii
children.text: has been convicted of an offense specified in .section 30304(a)(3) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: The State shall give full weight and consideration to that information in deciding whether to issue the individual a commercial driver’s license.
The State shall adopt and enforce regulations prescribed by the Secretary under as 31310(j) of this title.2So in original. Probably should be “section”.2

The State shall maintain, as part of its driver information system, a record of each violation of a State or local motor vehicle traffic control law while operating a motor vehicle (except a parking violation) for each individual who holds a commercial driver’s license. The record shall be available upon request to the individual, the Secretary, employers, prospective employers, State licensing and law enforcement agencies, and their authorized agents.

children.label: A
children.text: record in the driving record of an individual who has a commercial driver’s license issued by the State; and

children.label: B
children.text: make available to all authorized persons and governmental entities having access to such record,
The State shall revoke, suspend, or cancel the commercial driver’s license of an individual in accordance with regulations issued by the Secretary to carry out section 31310(g).
The State shall operate a commercial driver’s license information system that is compatible with the modernized commercial driver’s license information system under section 31309.

children.label: i
children.text: each conviction relating to the operation of a commercial motor vehicle; and

children.label: ii
children.text: each conviction relating to the operation of a non-commercial motor vehicle; and

children.label: B
children.text: for an unlicensed driver or a driver holding a foreign non-commercial driver’s license, each conviction relating to the operation of a commercial motor vehicle.

Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of the Commercial Motor Vehicle Safety Enhancement Act of 2012, the State shall implement a system and practices for the exclusive electronic exchange of driver history record information on the system the Secretary maintains under section 31309, including the posting of convictions, withdrawals, and disqualifications.

Before renewing or issuing a commercial driver’s license to an individual, the State shall request information pertaining to the individual from the drug and alcohol clearinghouse maintained under section 31306a.

Not later than 5 years after the date of enactment of the Commercial Motor Vehicle Safety Enhancement Act of 2012, the State shall establish and maintain, as part of its driver information system, the capability to receive an electronic copy of a medical examiner’s certificate, from a certified medical examiner, for each holder of a commercial driver’s license issued by the State who operates or intends to operate in interstate commerce.

children.heading: .—State Satisfaction of Requirements
children.label: b .—State Satisfaction of Requirements
children.text: A State may satisfy the requirements of subsection (a) of this section that the State disqualify an individual from operating a commercial motor vehicle by revoking, suspending, or canceling the driver’s license issued to the individual.

children.heading: .—Notification
children.label: c .—Notification
children.text: Not later than 30 days after being notified by a State of the proposed issuance of a commercial driver’s license to an individual, the Secretary or the operator of the information system under , as the case may be, shall notify the State whether the individual has a commercial driver’s license issued by another State or has been disqualified from operating a commercial motor vehicle by another State or the Secretary.section 31309 of this title

children.heading: State Commercial Driver’s License Program Plan.—
children.label: d State Commercial Driver’s License Program Plan.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A State shall submit a plan to the Secretary for complying with the requirements under this section during the period beginning on the date the plan is submitted and ending on .September 30, 2016

children.label: A
children.text: the actions that the State will take to address any deficiencies in the State’s commercial driver’s license program, as identified by the Secretary in the most recent audit of the program; and

children.label: B
children.text: other actions that the State will take to comply with the requirements under subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Implementation schedule
children.label: A .—Implementation schedule
children.text: A plan submitted by a State under paragraph (1) shall include a schedule for the implementation of the actions identified under paragraph (2). In establishing the schedule, the State shall prioritize actions to address any deficiencies highlighted by the Secretary as critical in the most recent audit of the program.

children.heading: .—Deadline for compliance with requirements
children.label: B .—Deadline for compliance with requirements
children.text: A plan submitted by a State under paragraph (1) shall include assurances that the State will take the necessary actions to comply with the requirements of subsection (a) not later than .September 30, 2015

children.label: A
children.text: review each plan submitted under paragraph (1);

children.label: i
children.text: approve a plan if the Secretary determines that the plan meets the requirements under this subsection and promotes the goals of this chapter; and

children.label: ii
children.text: disapprove a plan that the Secretary determines does not meet the requirements or does not promote the goals.

children.label: A
children.text: provide a written explanation of the disapproval to the State; and

children.label: B
children.text: allow the State to modify the plan and resubmit it for approval.

children.heading: .—Plan updates
children.label: 6 .—Plan updates
children.text: The Secretary may require a State to review and update a plan, as appropriate.

children.heading: .—Annual Comparison of State Levels of Compliance
children.label: e .—Annual Comparison of State Levels of Compliance

children.text: compare the relative levels of compliance by States with the requirements under subsection (a); and
make the results of the comparison available to the public.
